{{Quote|Golak nee schova Impay laash…|Tolga insults Major Birket|<ref name="IDC">''[[Imperial Double-Cross]]''</ref>}}
'''Tolga''' was a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] thug who worked as an enforcer for a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]]. He almost apprehended the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Blaine Hansom]] but the interference of [[Platt Okeefe/Legends|Platt Okeefe]] thwarted his capture. He later had a chance for revenge against Okeefe when he teamed up with the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boddu Bocck]]. However, he was again thwarted by outside inference when a young boy, [[Darrik]], shot at him.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Looks like you just bagged Tolga—he's a seedy Rodian hunter who's been giving me trouble for a while.|Okeefe to Darrik|<ref name="IDC" />}}
[[File:BocckBeylyssaTulga-IDC.png|thumb|left|200px|Beylyssa, Boddu Bocck, and Tolga]]
Tolga was a [[Color/Legends|green]] [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] who initially worked as an enforcer for a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]], and in that capacity, he tried to track down the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Blaine Hansom]]. He found him in a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] hiding out from the local [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] presence and would have apprehended him if not for the interference of [[Platt Okeefe/Legends|Platt Okeefe]] and [[Tru'eb Cholakk]]. The pair took pity on the inexperienced [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and forced Tolga to let Hansom go free.<ref name="RPG" />

After his employer's demise, Tolga was taken on by the bounty hunter [[Boddu Bocck]] as an apprentice. While his skills were lacking compared to Bocck and his associate [[Beylyssa]], Tolga provided some extra firepower for the more professional pair.<ref name="IDC" />

Tolga was present on [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] when [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Birket]] of the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] sought out Bocck. He insulted the major, who swiftly swung his [[Cane/Legends|walking stick]] up to the Rodian's throat, a small [[Knife/Legends|blade]] extending from the end. The major hired the bounty hunters to apprehend Platt Okeefe and Tru'eb Cholakk and to rescue the boy [[Darrik]], who was traveling with Okeefe. Okeefe and the boy were already en route to [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], and Bocck assigned Tolga and Beylyssa to capture them while he traveled to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to apprehend the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]].<ref name="IDC" />

After arriving on Bespin, Okeefe left her ship and Tolga trailed her. He kept watch as she met up with her supplier, [[Mussat Nasrabi]], at his [[Casino/Legends|casino]], [[The Floating Fish]]. Okeefe then descended to the lower levels of the city to obtain her cargo of [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas. While she tried to slice a lock, Tolga crept up behind her and prepared to shoot her with his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. However, his plan was foiled when Darrik shot him, alerting Okeefe to his presence. Together, the two escaped Bespin.<ref name="IDC" />

==Personality and traits==
Tolga wore a [[Blast vest/Legends|blast vest]] with a number of insignia on it.<ref name="RPG" /> As a bounty hunter, he used a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]], but he was not particularly skilled in his craft; Major Birket considered him to be only a low level enforcer whose skills were less developed than either of his sometime-partners, Bocck and Beylyssa.<ref name="IDC" /> He had a nasally voice.<ref name="RPG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tolga first appeared in the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'', in a short pick-a-path adventure to introduce the reader to role-playing. There, he was one of the player's protagonists. He later reappeared in ''[[Imperial Double-Cross]]'', a book-length a pick-a-path adventure, where the reader plays as Darrik. As such, a [[canon]]ical set of events has not been determined, yet both Tolga and Beylyssa must be defeated (with a number of possible ways) for the adventure to continue. It is possible for Tolga to [[Death/Legends|die]] in some of the outcomes.

==Appearances==
